title: Thelypteris rupestris
intro: Thelypteris rupestris, also known as rock fern, is a species of fern that is native to the Americas and belongs to the family Thelypteridaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its purported health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to its ability to aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, colds, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Thelypteris rupestris include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Thelypteris rupestris is often prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture to treat various health conditions, although its efficacy and safety have not been extensively scientifically studied.
taxonomy: Thelypteris rupestris is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. It is part of the genus Thelypteris, which comprises a group of ferns commonly known as the marsh ferns.
